Patch Set 1:

hmm seems you are not familiar with gerrit
you create a new gerrit instead of updating the first one
now you have 2 patchsets, we can merge this one and abandon the first one
but normally gerrit is a tool to track the change in one single patchset

if you start from your first patchet
- do your modification
- add them locally git add doc/docs_5g_a1_adaptor.rst docs/docs_usecases.rst
- then commit them in incremental mode: git commit --signoff --amend (the amend is important it means that you will amend the first patch, if you do not put amend, it will create a new one)
- git review => on the first patch we will get a patchset #2, that we can compare with #1 to see what is fixed or not

now that you create 2 patch proabbly from the same local branch, it is possible to come back butit is not straight forward.

so tell me if you want to give a try
or if you prefer that we validate this patch and abandon the first one (assuming that next time you will amend:))
Change has been successfully rebased and submitted as 287b75a69965d90e56dee2e6006fde931f9942d6 by Morgan Richomme
